HomePage.title: Applied AI Systems Lab
HomePage.description: Open-source AI playground for developers, showcasing production-grade patterns for safe, cost-aware multi-model orchestration, observability, and experiments like Model Arena and Lottery Probability Lab.

SettingsModal.privacyNote: Your keys are stored only in your browser's localStorage and never leave this device except when sent directly to the AI providers via our secure API endpoint.

metadata.title: Nguyen Van Bo | Frontend / Full-stack Engineer
metadata.description: Frontend / Full-stack Engineer with 6+ years experience in Fintech, SaaS, and Enterprise. Systems thinker, technical leader.

hero.role: Frontend / Full-stack Engineer | Systems Thinker | Technical Leader
hero.title: I design and build frontend systems that scale—in products, teams, and complexity.
hero.description:
6+ years experience building products across Fintech, SaaS, and Enterprise
Now focused on my own ventures: AI-powered platforms and data-driven products
Based in Vietnam, open to global/remote opportunities

philosophy.title: My Approach
philosophy.intro1: After six years building products across startups and enterprise environments, I've transitioned to building my own ventures. From AI-powered astrology platforms to market intelligence tools, I'm passionate about creating products that solve real problems at the intersection of technology and culture.
philosophy.intro2: The rise of AI has opened new possibilities for solo founders. I've learned to leverage LLMs not just as tools, but as core product differentiators—whether it's generating personalized horoscopes or democratizing market data for Vietnamese businesses.

clarity.title: Turning ambiguity into clarity
clarity.description: I specialize in taking vague requirements and transforming them into actionable technical approaches.

culture.title: Working across cultures and teams
culture.description: From Tokyo to Ho Chi Minh City to Mountain View, I bridge technical possibilities with business realities.

tradeoffs.title: Making informed trade-offs
tradeoffs.description: I evaluate solutions based on long-term maintainability, team ramp-up time, and business impact.

ai.title: AI as Collaborator
ai.description: I embrace AI as a collaborator that amplifies human creativity and judgment.

vnlocals.title: VN Locals
vnlocals.context: Platform connecting travelers with authentic local experiences in Vietnam
vnlocals.problem: Tourists struggle to find genuine local experiences beyond typical tourist attractions
vnlocals.constraints: Limited budget as solo founder, need to validate market quickly, build trust with both sides
vnlocals.decisions: Next.js with server components for SEO, Supabase for real-time features, simple booking flow

vnmarketinsights.title: VN Market Insights
vnmarketinsights.context: Data analytics platform providing market intelligence for Vietnam business landscape
vnmarketinsights.problem: Businesses lack accessible, affordable market data for Vietnam market entry decisions
vnmarketinsights.constraints: Data sourcing challenges, need to present complex data simply, bootstrap budget
vnmarketinsights.decisions: Python data pipelines, React with D3.js visualizations, freemium pricing model

mystar.title: MyStar.vn
mystar.context: AI-powered astrology platform generating personalized horoscopes and astrological insights
mystar.problem: Traditional astrology consultations are expensive and inaccessible to younger audiences
mystar.constraints: AI model accuracy, cultural sensitivity in astrology, building trust in automated readings
mystar.decisions: Fine-tuned LLM with astrology datasets, multilingual support, user feedback loop for improvement

theorientoracle.title: The Orient Oracle
theorientoracle.context: AI-driven oriental fortune telling platform combining traditional wisdom with modern technology
theorientoracle.problem: Preserving and modernizing traditional Eastern fortune telling practices for digital natives
theorientoracle.constraints: Balancing tradition with innovation, multiple fortune telling systems (Bazi, Zi Wei Dou Shu), AI hallucination risks
theorientoracle.decisions: Hybrid AI + expert validation system, educational content alongside predictions, community features

aiLab.title: Applied AI Playground
aiLab.description: My personal sandbox for exploring AI engineering—building practical AI integrations with cost tracking, observability, and intelligent caching. These demos show how I approach new technologies and build systems that can scale.

vietnamese-text.name: Vietnamese Text Analyzer
vietnamese-text.description: AI-powered sentiment analysis and tone detection for Vietnamese language

code-insight.name: Code Insight Engine
code-insight.description: Automated code complexity analysis and architecture pattern detection

sql-generator.name: SQL Generator
sql-generator.description: Natural language to SQL query conversion with multi-dialect support

data-extractor.name: Structured Data Extractor
data-extractor.description: Transform free-form text into strict, validated JSON using Zod.

model-arena.name: Model Arena
model-arena.description: Compare responses, cost, and latency across multiple AI models side-by-side.

intent-classifier.name: Smart Intent Classifier
intent-classifier.description: Convert user natural language into structured intents and actionable parameters.

robustness-tester.name: Prompt Robustness Tester
robustness-tester.description: Evaluate how AI handles noisy inputs, typos, and adversarial prompt variations.

cot-reasoning.name: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
cot-reasoning.description: Visualize the multi-step logical reasoning process for complex problem solving.

product.title: With Product & Design
product.content: I believe in collaborative discovery. I participate in user research, contribute to product strategy discussions, and help designers understand technical possibilities and constraints.

ambiguity.title: With Ambiguous Requirements
ambiguity.content: Vague requirements are opportunities for deeper understanding. I ask clarifying questions, create concrete examples, and propose small experiments to validate assumptions.

complex.title: With Complex Systems
complex.content: Debugging is building mental models of how systems interact. I map dependencies, trace data flows, and look for systemic issues rather than treating symptoms in isolation.

stakeholders.title: With Stakeholders
stakeholders.content: Technical excellence matters little if it doesn't solve real problems. I translate technical concepts into business outcomes and explain trade-offs in accessible terms.

ownership.title: With Ownership
ownership.content: I take responsibility beyond my immediate tasks—considering how my work affects other teams, documenting decisions for future maintainers, and proactively identifying potential issues.

ai.title: With AI Tools
ai.content: I embrace AI as a collaborator that amplifies human creativity. While AI accelerates prototyping, the uniquely human skills of systems thinking and strategic decision-making remain essential.

techStack.title: Tech Stack
techStack.disclaimer: These are tools I'm comfortable with, not a measure of my value or capabilities.

future.title: Future Direction
future.intro: I'm actively developing my skills in technical leadership, focusing on:

team.title: Team Development
team.description: Mentoring junior engineers, establishing best practices, creating psychological safety for experimentation and learning.

strategy.title: Strategic Thinking
strategy.description: Connecting technical decisions to business outcomes, evaluating long-term implications of architectural choices.

systems.title: Systems Leadership
systems.description: Building platforms that enable other teams to move faster, designing processes that scale, creating tools that multiply human effectiveness.

future.conclusion: As AI becomes more integrated into our workflows, I'm particularly interested in how engineering organizations can evolve to leverage these tools while preserving the human judgment that separates good solutions from great ones.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: I'm currently exploring opportunities for technical leadership roles—particularly those that involve shaping product direction, mentoring teams, and building systems that stand the test of time.
contact.location: Based in Vietnam, open to remote/global opportunities.

footer.role: Nguyen Van Bo • Frontend / Full-stack Engineer

vietnamese-text.textHelp: Provide Vietnamese text for sentiment analysis, tone detection, and more.
vietnamese-text.instructions: How to use: Paste any Vietnamese text into the box below. The AI will analyze sentiment, tone, and extract key phrases, providing a comprehensive summary.

code-insight.codeHelp: Submit code for complexity analysis, architecture review, and performance optimization suggestions.
code-insight.instructions: How to use: Select the programming language, paste your code snippet, and click Analyze. AI will review it for patterns, complexity, and potential improvements.

sql-generator.descriptionPlaceholder:
Describe the query you need in natural language...
Example: Get all users who signed up in the last 30 days and have made at least 3 orders
sql-generator.descriptionHelp: Describe what data you need, any conditions, sorting, or grouping requirements.
sql-generator.schemaPlaceholder:
Paste your table schema here...
Example:
users (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
orders (id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, total DECIMAL, created_at TIMESTAMP)
sql-generator.schemaHelp: Provide table definitions to get more accurate queries using your actual column names.
sql-generator.instructions: How to use: Describe the query you need in plain English. Optionally, provide your database schema (tables/columns) to help the AI generate more precise SQL.

lottery-probability-lab.poolSizeHelp: Total numbers to choose from. For Vietlott Power 6/55, n = 55.
lottery-probability-lab.pickCountHelp: How many numbers you pick on each ticket.
lottery-probability-lab.ticketPriceHelp: Face value of a single ticket in Vietnamese Dong.
lottery-probability-lab.jackpotHelp: Estimated jackpot size. Use a rough average if it varies over time.
lottery-probability-lab.drawsHelp: How many lottery drawings you hypothetically participate in (e.g. 104 draws ≈ 2 years at 2 draws/week).
lottery-probability-lab.ticketsPerDrawHelp: How many tickets you buy each time there is a draw.
lottery-probability-lab.instructions: How to use: Configure the lottery game (e.g. Vietlott Power 6/55), ticket price, jackpot size, and how many tickets you hypothetically buy over time. This tool computes exact odds and expected value to show how unlikely jackpots are and why lottery is mathematically negative-EV. It does not encourage real-world gambling.

data-extractor.schemaDescriptionPlaceholder: E.g. Extract person names, birth dates, and addresses...
data-extractor.textHelp: The text you want to transform into structured JSON.
data-extractor.schemaDescriptionHelp: Describe what entities you want the AI to look for.
data-extractor.instructions: How to use: Paste any unstructured text (email, article, etc.) into 'Source Text', then describe what you want to extract in 'Extraction Requirements'. The AI will return clean, structured JSON.

model-arena.promptRequestedPlaceholder: Enter the prompt to compare across models...
model-arena.promptRequestedHelp: This prompt will be sent to multiple models for comparison.
model-arena.instructions: How to use: Enter a 'Test Prompt' you want to benchmark. Select at least 2 models. You will see side-by-side responses with analysis of strengths and weaknesses for each model.

intent-classifier.userInputPlaceholder: E.g. Book a flight to London tomorrow...
intent-classifier.userInputHelp: Enter a natural language command for the AI to classify.
intent-classifier.instructions: How to use: Enter a natural user command. The AI will classify the intent, determine priority, and extract parameters needed to execute the action.

robustness-tester.basePromptPlaceholder: Enter the prompt you want to test for robustness...
robustness-tester.instructions: How to use: Enter a standard prompt. Select a 'Perturbation Type' (e.g., Typos). The AI will automatically alter your prompt and test if the model remains resilient and accurate.

cot-reasoning.problemPlaceholder: Enter a logic puzzle or complex problem...
cot-reasoning.instructions: How to use: Enter a logic puzzle or a complex problem. The AI will clearly visualize each step of its thought process before arriving at the final answer.

costEstimator.title: Estimated Cost
costEstimator.tooltip: Estimates cost based on input length + potential max output. Daily limit: ${limit}
costEstimator.overLimit: Exceeds limit! Shorten input or switch model.
costEstimator.sharedBudgetNote: Using the lab's shared budget: each request is capped at about ${perRequest} and there is a global daily limit of ${daily}.
costEstimator.byokNote: You are using your own API key (BYOK). The lab's shared budget limits (${daily}/day and about ${perRequest} per request) do not apply; charges are billed directly to your own provider account.

modelSelector.label: AI Model
modelSelector.help: Different models have different capabilities and costs.

actions.scanInstruction: Scan this QR code with your phone camera to open this result on mobile
actions.enterTitle: Enter a title for this favorite...

rate_limit.title: Too many requests
rate_limit.suggestion: Wait a moment, then try again. Limit is 5 requests per minute per IP.

budget.title: Daily budget reached
budget.suggestion: The daily spending limit has been reached. Try again tomorrow or adjust the limit in the server config.

validation.title: Invalid input
validation.suggestion: Check the form fields and fix the errors below.

api_key.title: API configuration
api_key.suggestion: Add an API key in Settings (gear icon) or use your own key (BYOK).

server.title: Something went wrong
server.suggestion: Try again in a moment or check your connection.

lottery.disclaimerBody: This tool is for educational and research purposes only. It uses basic probability theory to illustrate how unlikely lottery jackpots are and why the expected value is negative in most real-world games. It does not provide gambling advice, does not guarantee any outcome, and should not be used to make financial or betting decisions. Always comply with local laws and only participate in lottery games, if you choose to, in a legal and responsible way using money you can afford to lose.
lottery.historySubtitle: Counts of how often each number 1–55 has been drawn historically. In a fair lottery this distribution should be roughly flat; past frequency does not increase the true probability of a number in future draws.

what.heading: What is this?
what.content: This is a developer-centric sandbox for testing production-grade AI integration patterns. Each experiment demonstrates a specific use case, complete with cost estimation, performance metrics, and caching strategies.

quickStart.heading: Quick Start
quickStart.content:
1. Pick an experiment from the left.
2. Adjust the settings or use the default input.
3. Hit 'Analyze' to see the AI in action.
4. Explore the 'Analytics' and 'History' tags to see what happened under the hood.

whatYouGet.heading: What you get
whatYouGet.content: Every request is fully instrumented. You get side-by-side results, token counts, latency breakdowns, and estimated costs. Plus, we show you the 'Thought Process' for most experiments.

explained.heading: Experiments Explained
explained.content: Choose from tools like the SQL Generator, Code Insight Engine, or Structured Data Extractor. Each one is built with a specific architecture pattern (e.g., Zod validation, Prompt versioning).

tips.heading: Pro Tips
tips.content:
• Use your own API keys in Settings to bypass daily limits.
• Check the 'View Architecture' page to see how the system is built.
• Most results can be copied or shared with others.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Analyzes Vietnamese text for sentiment (Positive/Negative/Neutral), detects tone, and extracts key keywords relevant to the context.

how.heading: How to use
how.content: Paste any Vietnamese text (comments, articles, etc.). The AI uses language-specific prompt engineering to understand nuances and dialects.

examples.heading: Try these
examples.content:
• 'Dùng rất ổn, giá cả phải chăng.'
• 'Chất lượng quá kém, không bao giờ quay lại!'

cost.heading: Cost efficiency
cost.content: This model is highly optimized. We recommend using 'gpt-4o-mini' for high volume sentiment analysis to save costs while maintaining accuracy.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Deeply analyzes code architecture, detects smells, and provides actionable refactoring suggestions.

how.heading: How to use
how.content: Select your language and paste a function or class. The AI evaluates complexity and suggests design pattern improvements.

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices
bestPractices.content: Submit logical units (like a single class or complex function) rather than raw files for the best accuracy.

cost.heading: Context tips
cost.content: Large files consume more tokens. Use 'Claude 3.5 Sonnet' for complex architecture logic as it has a larger context window.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Converts natural language descriptions into valid, dialect-specific SQL queries.

how.heading: How to use
how.content: Describe your goal (e.g., 'Get top 5 customers'). Adding your schema (table definitions) makes the output significantly more accurate.

examples.heading: Examples
examples.content:
• 'Find users who signed up last week'
• 'Calculate average revenue per month per category'

schema.heading: Schema Power
schema.content: If you provide table names and columns, the AI will use your actual database structure instead of guessing.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Turns messy, unstructured text into clean JSON data based on your requirements.

how.heading: How it works
how.content: We use a 'Strict Output' pattern. This ensures the result matches your JSON schema perfectly, or it returns an error.

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices
bestPractices.content: Be specific in your 'Extraction Requirements'. Mention data types (e.g., 'extract dates as ISO strings').

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Runs the same prompt across multiple models side-by-side to compare latency, cost, and quality.

how.heading: Why use it?
how.content: Ideal for benchmarking during development. You can see which model gives the best response for your specific prompt before committing.

useCases.heading: Use Cases
useCases.content: Compare GPT-4o vs Claude 3.5 vs Llama 3 to find the sweet spot between intelligence and price.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Determines the 'Intent' behind a user command and extracts the necessary action parameters.

how.heading: How to use
how.content: Perfect for building chat bots or voice assistants. It maps natural language (e.g., 'Book a ride') to a code-friendly action (e.g., 'BOOK_VEHICLE').

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Tests if your AI prompts are 'bulletproof' by injecting noise, typos, and adversarial variations.

how.heading: Why it matters
how.content: In production, users don't always type perfectly. This tool helps you ensure your AI remains reliable even with messy input.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Forces the AI to 'think out loud' step-by-step before giving the final answer.

how.heading: Why use it?
how.content: Improves accuracy for logic puzzles, math, and complex decision making. It makes the AI's reasoning fully transparent to you.

what.heading: What it does
what.content: Uses exact combinatorics (C(n, k)) and basic probability to quantify how unlikely lottery jackpots are, and to compute the expected value of different play scenarios.

how.heading: How to use
how.content: Pick a lottery type (e.g. Vietlott Power 6/55), set ticket price, jackpot size and how many tickets you hypothetically buy over time. The tool shows jackpot odds, expected value per ticket and the probability of hitting at least one jackpot over all draws.

bestPractices.heading: Important disclaimer
bestPractices.content: All valid combinations in a fair lottery have exactly the same probability. This lab does not generate 'better' or 'luckier' numbers, and it is not gambling advice. It is meant to build intuition about risk and expected value so that people understand lottery as paid entertainment, not an investment.

footer.tip: Tip: You can use these patterns in your own apps!
